aliases: St. Cyril, Bishop of Jerusalem and Doctor of the Church
shortBio: Fourth-century bishop of Jerusalem, teacher of the catechumens, and Doctor of the Church who defended Nicene faith during a period of intense doctrinal conflict.
quote: The dragon sits by the side of the road, watching those who pass. Beware lest he devour you. We go to the Father of Souls, but it is necessary to pass by the dragon.
patronages: Cyril is especially honored as a teacher of catechumens and a guide for those entering the sacramental life of the Church.

shortBio: French bishop of Grenoble elected in his twenties before ordination, remembered for carrying out Gregorian reform, preaching with clarity, caring generously for the poor, and helping Saint Bruno establish La Grande Chartreuse.
patronages: Hugh is especially honored as a reforming bishop and as an early supporter of the Carthusian vocation at La Grande Chartreuse.

canonicalName: St. Joseph, Spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary, Patron of the Universal Church
aliases: Joseph, Spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary
shortBio: Foster father of Jesus, spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary, and protector of the Holy Family, venerated throughout the Church as a model of obedience, work, and hidden fidelity.
patronages: He is patron of the universal Church, fathers, workers, families, a happy death, and all who seek faithful service in hidden life.

shortBio: Third-century Roman deacon and martyr, remembered for steadfast charity to the poor and fearless witness during the Valerian persecution.
patronages: He is honored as patron of deacons, the poor, cooks, chefs, firefighters, and those who serve the Church's works of charity.

shortBio: Second-century bishop of Corinth known through quotations preserved by Eusebius, remembered for strengthening churches with pastoral letters and encouraging unity in charity.
quote: From the beginning, it is your custom to bestow your alms in all places, and to furnish subsistence to many churches.
patronages: No specific universal patronage is securely attached to Dionysius of Corinth in the principal Catholic sources used by the app.

shortBio: Fourth-century bishop of Le Mans, honored for pastoral leadership, holiness of life, and the enduring devotion that later linked his cult to Paderborn as well as Le Mans.
quote: Lord, if your people still need me, I am ready for the task; your will be done.
patronages: He is honored as a patron of the dioceses of Le Mans and Paderborn and is traditionally invoked by those suffering from stones or related illnesses.

shortBio: Italian foundress of the Canossian Daughters of Charity, remembered for serving the poor and forming communities dedicated to education, catechesis, and works of mercy.
quote: Those who love are never tired, since love knows no burden.
patronages: She is especially honored within the Canossian family and among ministries devoted to education, catechesis, and service to the poor.

aliases: St.John Baptist de la Salle, priest,Founder of Brothers of the Christian schools
Saint John Baptist de La Salle
shortBio: French priest, founder of the Brothers of the Christian Schools, and major reformer of Catholic education who dedicated his life to the Christian formation of the poor.
quote: You carry out a work that requires you to touch hearts, but this you cannot do except by the Spirit of God.
patronages: He is the patron saint of teachers, school principals, and all who work in Catholic education.

shortBio: Fifth-century bishop and missionary to Ireland, remembered for preaching the Gospel with apostolic zeal and helping establish the faith throughout the island.
quote: I arise today through a mighty strength, the invocation of the Trinity.
patronages: He is honored as patron of Ireland and is especially invoked in missionary memory and Irish Catholic devotion.

shortBio: Sixth-century abbot, father of Western monasticism, and author of the Rule of Saint Benedict, whose wisdom shaped Christian prayer, work, and community life for centuries.
quote: Prefer nothing whatever to Christ.
patronages: He is patron of Europe, of monks, and of those seeking ordered prayer, work, and stability in Christian life.

shortBio: Franciscan priest and Doctor of the Church, renowned preacher, teacher of Scripture, and tireless servant of the poor whose preaching drew many back to the faith.
quote: Actions speak louder than words; let your words teach and your actions speak.
patronages: He is widely honored as patron of the poor, lost things, and those searching for what has been misplaced in body or soul.

shortBio: Italian founder of the Franciscan family, marked by evangelical poverty, love of Christ crucified, and joyful reverence for all creation.
patronages: He is patron of animals, ecology, and all who seek to follow Christ in poverty, peace, and praise.

shortBio: Companion of Saint Francis and foundress of the Poor Clares, remembered for her life of Eucharistic devotion, poverty, and steadfast contemplative prayer.
quote: Love God, serve God; everything is in that.
patronages: She is honored as patron saint of television, eye disease, and contemplative communities rooted in poverty and Eucharistic love.

shortBio: Spanish priest and founder of the Order of Preachers, devoted to truth, preaching, study, and the conversion of souls through charity and sound doctrine.
quote: Arm yourself with prayer rather than a sword; wear humility rather than fine clothes.
patronages: He is especially honored as patron of preachers, theologians, and those called to defend the faith through study and apostolic preaching.

shortBio: Bishop and Doctor of the Church whose conversion, preaching, and theological writings shaped Western Christianity and remain central to Catholic thought.
quote: You have made us for yourself, O Lord, and our hearts are restless until they rest in you.
patronages: He is especially honored among theologians, teachers, preachers, and all who seek God through repentance, grace, and truth.

shortBio: Mother of Saint Augustine, remembered for persevering prayer, patient tears, and steadfast hope for the conversion of her husband and son.
quote: Nothing is far from God.
patronages: She is honored as patron of wives, mothers, and parents who pray for children or loved ones who have wandered from the faith.

shortBio: Dominican friar and Doctor of the Church whose theological and philosophical synthesis remains one of the great intellectual treasures of Catholic tradition.
quote: To one who has faith, no explanation is necessary. To one without faith, no explanation is possible.
patronages: He is patron of Catholic schools, students, theologians, and all who seek wisdom through the harmony of faith and reason.

aliases: St. Therese of the Child Jesus
Saint Therese of the Child Jesus
shortBio: Carmelite nun and Doctor of the Church whose 'little way' taught holiness through confidence, hidden sacrifice, and childlike trust in God's merciful love.
quote: My vocation is love! In the heart of the Church, my Mother, I will be love.
patronages: She is patron of missions, missionaries, florists, and all who seek holiness through hidden fidelity and trusting love.

shortBio: Carmelite reformer, mystic, and Doctor of the Church who renewed religious life and left enduring teaching on prayer, interior conversion, and friendship with Christ.
quote: Let nothing disturb you, let nothing frighten you. All things are passing; God never changes. Patience obtains all things. Whoever has God lacks nothing. God alone suffices.
patronages: She is especially honored among those devoted to prayer, contemplative renewal, and the reform of religious life.

shortBio: Polish pope and saint whose long pontificate called the Church to holiness, mercy, courageous witness, and a renewed proclamation of Christ to the modern world.
quote: Do not be afraid. Open wide the doors to Christ.
patronages: He is especially honored in devotion connected with families, young people, Divine Mercy, and the new evangelization.

shortBio: Capuchin priest marked by deep prayer, devotion to confession and the Eucharist, and extraordinary suffering borne in union with Christ.
quote: Pray, hope, and don't worry.
patronages: He is especially honored among the faithful devoted to confession, Eucharistic reparation, patient suffering, and trust in God's mercy.

shortBio: Disciple of the Lord who stood near the Cross and became the first witness of the Resurrection, honored by the Church as the apostle to the apostles.
patronages: She is especially honored as a patron of penitents, contemplatives, and all who seek the risen Christ with faithful love.

shortBio: Dominican tertiary and Doctor of the Church whose intense prayer, bold counsel, and charity made her one of the most influential saints of the late medieval Church.
quote: Be who God meant you to be and you will set the world on fire.
patronages: She is patron of Europe, of Italy, nurses, and all who seek courage in prayer, truth, and service to the Church.

shortBio: Carmelite reformer, mystic, and Doctor of the Church whose teaching on purification, contemplation, and divine union remains central to Catholic spirituality.
quote: In the evening of life, we will be judged on love alone.
patronages: He is especially honored among contemplatives, poets, and those seeking to persevere through spiritual darkness toward union with God.

shortBio: Founder of the Society of Jesus, master of discernment, and spiritual guide whose conversion turned a soldier's ambition into apostolic service for the greater glory of God.
quote: Take, Lord, and receive all my liberty, my memory, my understanding, and my entire will.
patronages: He is patron of spiritual retreats, discernment, and all who seek to serve Christ with disciplined generosity and apostolic zeal.

shortBio: Jesuit missionary and companion of Saint Ignatius, remembered for carrying the Gospel across Asia with extraordinary courage, hardship, and joy.
quote: It is not the actual physical exertion that counts towards one's progress, nor the nature of the task, but by the spirit of faith with which it is undertaken.
patronages: He is patron of missions, missionaries, and all who labor to preach Christ across cultures and great distances.

shortBio: Foundress of the Missionaries of Charity, remembered for serving Christ in the poorest of the poor with radical simplicity, perseverance, and love.
quote: If you judge people, you have no time to love them.
patronages: She is especially honored among those serving the poor, the dying, the abandoned, and all who seek Christ in hidden acts of mercy.

shortBio: Polish nun and mystic whose Diary spread the Divine Mercy devotion throughout the Church and called sinners to trust in Jesus with confidence.
quote: Jesus, I trust in You.
patronages: She is especially honored among the faithful devoted to Divine Mercy, trust in Christ, and prayer for sinners.

shortBio: Conventual Franciscan priest and martyr of charity who offered his life in Auschwitz in place of another prisoner and witnessed to Christ through total consecration to Mary.
quote: The most deadly poison of our time is indifference.
patronages: He is honored as patron of families, prisoners, journalists, and all who seek to answer hatred with sacrificial love.

shortBio: Parish priest of Ars, famed confessor, and model of pastoral holiness whose hidden village ministry became a beacon for repentance and Eucharistic devotion.
quote: Private prayer is like straw scattered here and there: if you set it on fire, it makes a lot of little flames. But gather these straws into a bundle and light them, and you get a mighty fire.
patronages: He is patron of parish priests and is especially honored in devotion tied to confession, Eucharistic adoration, and priestly holiness.

shortBio: Italian physician, wife, and mother who witnessed to the sanctity of life through professional charity, family love, and heroic maternal sacrifice.
quote: The secret of happiness is to live moment by moment and to thank God for all that He, in His goodness, sends to us day after day.
patronages: She is especially honored among mothers, physicians, and pro-life Catholics seeking holiness in family and professional life.

shortBio: Sudanese-born religious sister whose life moved from slavery and suffering to freedom, forgiveness, and serene witness in Christ.
quote: If I were to meet the slave traders who kidnapped me and even those who tortured me, I would kneel and kiss their hands, for if that did not happen, I would not be a Christian and Religious today.
patronages: She is especially honored among victims of trafficking, the displaced, and all who seek healing, forgiveness, and freedom in Christ.

shortBio: American wife, mother, convert, and foundress whose faith, suffering, and educational work made her a foundational figure in the Catholic life of the United States.
quote: The first end I propose in our daily work is to do the will of God; secondly, to do it in the manner He wills; and thirdly, to do it because it is His will.
patronages: She is honored as patron of Catholic schools, widows, converts, and all who seek holiness in family life and education.

shortBio: Mohawk-Algonquin virgin and convert whose prayer, penance, and purity of heart made her the first canonized Native American saint in the Catholic Church.
quote: Who will teach me what is most pleasing to God, that I may do it?
patronages: She is especially honored among Native peoples, the natural world, and all who seek holiness through purity, prayer, and sacrificial love.

aliases: Saint Teresa Benedicta of the Cross
St. Teresa Benedicta of the Cross
shortBio: Jewish philosopher, Carmelite nun, martyr, and Doctoral intellect whose conversion to Christ and death at Auschwitz joined contemplative depth to heroic witness.
quote: Do not accept anything as the truth if it lacks love. And do not accept anything as love which lacks truth.
patronages: She is patron of Europe and is especially honored among philosophers, converts, and those who seek truth through intellect, prayer, and sacrifice.

shortBio: Peruvian lay Dominican and mystic whose hidden life of penance, charity, and intense love of Christ made her the first canonized saint of the Americas.
quote: Apart from the cross there is no other ladder by which we may get to heaven.
patronages: She is patron of Latin America, Peru, gardeners, and those who seek holiness through hidden sacrifice and love of the poor.

shortBio: Italian priest and founder who gave his life to the Christian formation of poor and abandoned youth through education, affection, discipline, and joy.
quote: It is not enough to love children; they must know they are loved.
patronages: He is patron of youth, apprentices, schoolchildren, and all who labor in Christian education and pastoral care for the young.

shortBio: Roman priest of joy and prayer whose humility, wit, and spiritual fatherhood renewed countless souls in the heart of the city.
quote: Cast yourself into the arms of God and be very sure that if He wants anything of you, He will fit you for the work and give you strength.
patronages: He is especially honored among parish clergy, joyful evangelizers, and those who seek holiness through humility, friendship, and cheerful penance.

shortBio: Apostle and evangelist, called from the tax booth by Christ, whose Gospel bears witness to Jesus as the promised Messiah and teacher of the Kingdom.
quote: Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
patronages: He is patron of accountants, bankers, tax collectors, and all who find in Christ the call to leave sin behind and follow him.

shortBio: Evangelist, companion of Saint Paul, and physician whose Gospel and Acts of the Apostles offer one of the Church's richest portraits of mercy, mission, and the Holy Spirit.
quote: For nothing will be impossible with God.
patronages: He is patron of physicians, surgeons, artists, and all who serve the Gospel through healing, careful witness, and sacred art.

shortBio: Evangelist traditionally linked to Saint Peter, whose Gospel proclaims Jesus Christ with urgency, clarity, and a vivid sense of apostolic witness.
quote: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
patronages: He is patron of notaries, interpreters, and all who labor to proclaim the Gospel with courage and clarity.

shortBio: Apostle, evangelist, and beloved disciple of the Lord, remembered for contemplative depth, fidelity at the Cross, and profound witness to the Word made flesh.
quote: God is love.
patronages: He is honored as patron of theologians, writers, friendships of spiritual fidelity, and all who seek to remain close to the heart of Christ.

shortBio: Apostle of the risen Lord whose path from doubt to confession gave the Church one of its clearest proclamations of Christ's divinity.
quote: My Lord and my God!
patronages: He is honored as patron of builders, architects, and all who seek faith through honest questioning brought into the presence of Christ.

shortBio: Apostle of Christ remembered in Catholic devotion as a powerful intercessor in desperate cases and a faithful witness to perseverance in the love of God.
quote: Keep yourselves in the love of God, waiting for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ unto eternal life.
patronages: He is patron of desperate causes and hopeless situations, and is widely invoked by the faithful in times of great need.

shortBio: Archangel and leader of the heavenly host, honored in the Church as defender of God's glory and protector against the powers of evil.
quote: Who is like God?
patronages: He is patron of soldiers, police, first responders, and all who seek heavenly protection in spiritual combat.

canonicalName: Saint Joseph, Spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary, Patron of the Universal Church
aliases: Joseph, Spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary
shortBio: Foster father of Jesus, spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary, and protector of the Holy Family, venerated throughout the Church as a model of obedience, work, and hidden fidelity.
patronages: He is patron of the universal Church, fathers, workers, families, a happy death, and all who seek faithful service in hidden life.

shortBio: Chief of the apostles, first among the Twelve, and shepherd entrusted by Christ to strengthen his brethren and feed the flock.
quote: Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life.
patronages: He is honored as patron of the papacy, fishermen, and all who seek courage to confess Christ after weakness and repentance.

shortBio: Apostle to the Gentiles whose dramatic conversion and tireless missionary labor made him one of the Church's greatest preachers, theologians, and martyrs.
quote: It is no longer I who live, but Christ who lives in me.
patronages: He is honored as patron of missionaries, evangelists, writers, and all who seek bold conversion and fearless proclamation of Christ.

shortBio: Archangel of annunciation and divine messages, honored in the Church as the heavenly messenger who proclaimed decisive moments in salvation history.
quote: Hail, full of grace, the Lord is with you.
patronages: He is honored as patron of messengers, communications, broadcasters, and all who seek to receive and deliver God's word faithfully.

shortBio: Archangel of healing and guidance, known especially through the Book of Tobit as companion of travelers and minister of God's restorative mercy.
quote: I am Raphael, one of the seven angels who stand and serve before the Glory of the Lord.
patronages: He is honored as patron of travelers, the blind, physicians, and all who seek healing and safe guidance under God's providence.

shortBio: Apostle of the Lord, brother of John, and one of the inner circle who witnessed key moments of Christ's ministry before sealing his witness in martyrdom.
patronages: He is honored as patron of pilgrims, Spain, and all who follow Christ with apostolic courage and readiness for sacrifice.

shortBio: Apostolic leader of the Church in Jerusalem, remembered for holiness, prudence, and steadfast witness in the earliest years of the Christian community.
quote: Be doers of the word, and not hearers only.
patronages: He is especially honored among those devoted to practical holiness, integrity of life, and steadfast leadership in the Church.

shortBio: First-called apostle and brother of Peter, remembered for bringing others to Christ and following the Lord with generous readiness.
quote: Hail, precious Cross, that hast been consecrated by the Body of my Lord.
patronages: He is honored as patron of fishermen, Scotland, and all who seek to lead others to Christ with humble apostolic zeal.

shortBio: Apostle of Christ traditionally identified with Nathanael, remembered for frankness before the Lord and faithful witness unto martyrdom.
patronages: He is honored as patron of those in difficult trades and of the faithful who seek sincerity, courage, and apostolic endurance.

shortBio: One of the Twelve apostles, remembered in the Church's tradition as a faithful though quiet witness to Christ and the apostolic mission.
quote: The Lord sends us forth as lambs among wolves.
patronages: He is especially honored with Saint Jude in apostolic devotion and among those who labor faithfully without seeking prominence.

shortBio: Apostle chosen to take the place of Judas after the Ascension, bearing witness with the Eleven to the Resurrection of Christ.
quote: It is necessary that one of these men become a witness with us of His resurrection.
patronages: He is honored among those called to faithful service after loss, transition, or restoration within the life of the Church.

shortBio: Bishop and Doctor of the Church who defended the divinity of Christ with remarkable courage during the Arian crisis and helped shape the Church's confession of the Incarnation.
quote: God became man so that man might become God.
patronages: He is especially honored among theologians, bishops, and all who seek steadfastness in defending the truth of Christ.

shortBio: Bishop, monk, and Doctor of the Church whose theological clarity, liturgical influence, and care for the poor made him one of the great pillars of Eastern Christianity.
quote: A tree is known by its fruit; a man by his deeds.
patronages: He is especially honored among monastics, theologians, and all who unite doctrinal truth with practical charity.

shortBio: Pope and Doctor of the Church whose pastoral wisdom, liturgical influence, missionary vision, and spiritual teaching shaped Christian life for centuries.
quote: The proof of love is in the works. Where love exists, it works great things.
patronages: He is especially honored among teachers, liturgical musicians, pastors, and those entrusted with spiritual governance in the Church.

shortBio: Priest, scholar, and Doctor of the Church whose biblical learning and translation work made him one of the central figures in the Church's life of Scripture.
quote: Ignorance of Scripture is ignorance of Christ.
patronages: He is patron of biblical scholars, translators, librarians, and all who seek Christ through diligent study of Scripture.

shortBio: Bishop and Doctor of the Church whose preaching, hymnody, and fearless pastoral leadership shaped the Latin Church and deeply influenced Saint Augustine.
quote: No one heals himself by wounding another.
patronages: He is especially honored among bishops, teachers, and those who seek courage in pastoral leadership and sacred song.

shortBio: Bishop, martyr, and Doctor of the Church whose defense of apostolic tradition and the goodness of creation remains foundational for Catholic theology.
quote: The glory of God is man fully alive.
patronages: He is especially honored among theologians and those who defend the apostolic faith against confusion and false teaching.

shortBio: Monk, archbishop, and Doctor of the Church remembered for uniting contemplative prayer with rigorous theological reflection in the service of the truth.
quote: I do not seek to understand in order to believe, but I believe in order to understand.
patronages: He is especially honored among theologians, philosophers, and all who seek the harmony of prayerful faith and disciplined thought.

shortBio: Cistercian abbot and Doctor of the Church whose preaching, Marian devotion, and theological depth made him one of the most influential voices of the medieval West.
quote: You wish to see; listen. Hearing is a step toward vision.
patronages: He is especially honored among monks, contemplatives, and those devoted to the Blessed Virgin Mary and the interior life.

shortBio: Franciscan theologian, cardinal, and Doctor of the Church whose writings unite contemplative love, theological depth, and a profoundly Christ-centered vision of creation and redemption.
quote: In beautiful things Saint Francis saw Beauty itself, and through His vestiges imprinted on creation he followed his Beloved everywhere.
patronages: He is especially honored among theologians, Franciscans, and those who seek wisdom through prayerful contemplation of Christ.

shortBio: Dominican bishop, scholar, and Doctor of the Church remembered for extraordinary breadth of learning and for teaching Saint Thomas Aquinas.
quote: Do you wish to study to your advantage? Let devotion accompany all your studies.
patronages: He is patron of scientists, philosophers, and students who seek truth through disciplined study ordered to God.

shortBio: Jesuit cardinal and Doctor of the Church whose theological precision and pastoral clarity made him one of the leading Catholic voices of the Counter-Reformation.
quote: The school of Christ is the school of charity.
patronages: He is especially honored among catechists, apologists, canon lawyers, and all who defend the faith with learning joined to charity.

shortBio: Jesuit priest and Doctor of the Church whose catechisms, preaching, and educational work strengthened Catholic life in lands shaken by the Reformation.
quote: If you have too much to do, with God's help you will find time to do it all.
patronages: He is especially honored among catechists, teachers, and all who labor to hand on Catholic doctrine with clarity and patience.

shortBio: Bishop, spiritual director, and Doctor of the Church whose gentle wisdom made holiness accessible to laypeople, clergy, and religious alike.
quote: Have patience with all things, but first of all with yourself.
patronages: He is patron of writers, journalists, the Catholic press, and all who seek holiness through gentleness, patience, and fidelity in daily life.

shortBio: Bishop, moral theologian, founder, and Doctor of the Church whose teaching on prayer, conscience, and redemption shaped Catholic spirituality and pastoral care.
quote: He who prays is certainly saved; he who does not pray is certainly damned.
patronages: He is especially honored among confessors, moral theologians, and all who seek mercy, prayer, and confidence in Christ the Redeemer.

shortBio: Missionary preacher and spiritual writer whose Marian teaching and call to total consecration to Jesus through Mary left a lasting mark on Catholic devotion.
quote: God wishes that His holy Mother should be at present more known, more loved, more honored than she has ever been.
patronages: He is especially honored among Marian devotees, missionaries, and all who seek deeper consecration to Christ through the Blessed Virgin Mary.

shortBio: Theologian, pastor, cardinal, and convert whose intellectual honesty and spiritual depth made him one of the most important Catholic voices of the modern era.
quote: To live is to change, and to be perfect is to have changed often.
patronages: He is especially honored among converts, educators, students, and all who seek truth with humility, conscience, and perseverance.

shortBio: American heiress turned foundress whose life of Eucharistic devotion and sacrificial charity served Black and Native communities through schools, missions, and religious life.
quote: If we wish to serve God and love our neighbor well, we must manifest our joy in the service we render to Him and them.
patronages: She is especially honored among philanthropists, racial justice ministries, Catholic educators, and all who seek holiness through generous service of the marginalized.

shortBio: Missionary priest who gave his life among those with leprosy on Molokai, witnessing to Christ through fearless proximity, pastoral care, and solidarity with the abandoned.
quote: I make myself a leper with the lepers to gain all to Jesus Christ.
patronages: He is honored among those suffering from leprosy, people with stigmatized illnesses, missionaries, and all who serve the abandoned with sacrificial love.

shortBio: Religious sister and foundress whose practical charity, administrative strength, and fearless service transformed care for the sick and abandoned in Hawaii.
quote: I am not afraid of any disease, hence it will be my greatest delight even to minister to the abandoned lepers.
patronages: She is especially honored among nurses, hospital workers, religious sisters, and all who serve the sick without fear.

shortBio: Holy Cross brother of humble station whose deep devotion to Saint Joseph and hidden ministry of welcome, prayer, and healing made him one of Canada's most beloved saints.
quote: When I joined this community, the superiors showed me the door, and I remained forty years.
patronages: He is especially honored among doorkeepers, the sick, devotees of Saint Joseph, and all who seek holiness through humble service.

shortBio: Hermit, missionary, and witness of hidden fraternity whose contemplative life in the desert became a call to universal brotherhood rooted in the Eucharistic presence of Christ.
quote: I want to accustom all the inhabitants, Christians, Muslims, Jews, and pagans, to look on me as their brother — the universal brother.
patronages: He is especially honored among hermits, missionaries, and all who seek contemplative union with Christ expressed through radical fraternity.

shortBio: Carmelite priest, scholar, and martyr of conscience who resisted Nazi ideology with intellectual clarity, pastoral courage, and fidelity unto death.
quote: Those who want to win the world for Christ must have the courage to come into conflict with it.
patronages: He is especially honored among journalists, professors, and all who defend truth and human dignity against totalitarian lies.

shortBio: Priest and founder whose preaching emphasized the universal call to holiness through ordinary work, daily duties, and union with Christ in the secular world.
quote: Don't let your life be barren. Be useful. Make yourself felt. Shine forth with the torch of your faith and your love.
patronages: He is especially honored among working people, professionals, and all who seek sanctity in ordinary labor and family life.

shortBio: Priest of immense practical charity whose work for the poor, the sick, and the formation of clergy made him one of the Church's great organizers of mercy.
quote: Charity is certainly greater than any rule. Moreover, all rules must lead to charity.
patronages: He is honored as patron of charitable societies, works of mercy, hospitals, and all who serve Christ in the poor.

shortBio: Benedictine abbess, mystic, composer, and Doctor of the Church whose visionary writings and holistic wisdom made her one of the most remarkable women of the medieval Church.
quote: The Word is living, being, spirit, all verdant greening, all creativity.
patronages: She is especially honored among musicians, writers, natural healers, and all who seek contemplative wisdom ordered toward God's living creation.

shortBio: Wife, mother, widow, mystic, and foundress whose revelations and pilgrim witness made her a powerful voice of reform and devotion in the late medieval Church.
quote: The world would have peace if only men of politics would follow the Gospel.
patronages: She is patron of Europe, widows, and pilgrims, and is especially honored among those who seek holiness through family life, repentance, and contemplative fidelity.

shortBio: Visitation nun whose mystical experiences and fidelity to the Sacred Heart devotion helped spread one of the Church's most beloved forms of reparation and love to Christ.
quote: Behold this Heart which has so loved men that it has spared nothing.
patronages: She is especially honored among devotees of the Sacred Heart, those seeking reparation, and all who turn to the merciful love of Christ.

shortBio: Co-foundress with Saint Vincent de Paul of the Daughters of Charity, remembered for practical wisdom, interior surrender, and tireless service to the poor.
quote: The love of God finds us where we are and takes us where we need to go.
patronages: She is especially honored among social workers, charitable sisters, and all who seek to unite organized service with interior trust in God.

shortBio: Humble visionary of Lourdes whose simplicity, suffering, and fidelity to Our Lady's message made her one of the best-known saints of modern Catholic devotion.
quote: I shall spend every moment loving.
patronages: She is especially honored among the sick, the poor, and pilgrims, and is closely associated with healing, penance, and the Lourdes devotion.

shortBio: Lay Italian mystic whose intense love of the Passion, Eucharistic devotion, and hidden suffering made her a striking witness of union with the Crucified Christ.
quote: Let us go forward in peace, our eyes upon heaven, the only one goal of our labors.
patronages: She is especially honored among those devoted to the Passion of Christ, purity of heart, and patient endurance in illness and suffering.

shortBio: Young martyr of purity and forgiveness whose dying mercy toward her attacker became one of the clearest modern witnesses to chastity and Christian pardon.
quote: I forgive him, and I want him to be with me in Paradise.
patronages: She is especially honored among youth, victims of violence, and all who seek purity, forgiveness, and courage under trial.

shortBio: Foundress of the Little Sisters of the Poor whose hidden humility and radical trust in Providence shaped a worldwide work of loving care for the elderly poor.
quote: It is so beautiful to be poor, to have nothing, to await all from God.
patronages: She is especially honored among caregivers of the elderly, the poor, and all who seek holiness through hidden humility and confidence in Providence.

shortBio: Early Roman virgin martyr whose fearless fidelity to Christ made her one of the Church's most beloved witnesses to purity, courage, and total belonging to the Lord.
quote: Christ is my Spouse. He chose me first and His I will be.
patronages: She is especially honored among girls, those consecrated to chastity, and all who seek courage and purity under trial.

shortBio: Virgin martyr of the early Church whose name became inseparably linked with sacred music, praise, and steadfast confession of Christ.
quote: Come, soldiers of Christ, throw away the works of darkness and put on the armor of light.
patronages: She is patron of musicians, church music, singers, and all who desire to praise God with purity of heart.

shortBio: Virgin martyr of Syracuse whose light-filled witness and steadfast purity made her one of the most venerated saints of the early Church.
quote: Those who live in purity are temples of the Holy Spirit.
patronages: She is especially honored among the blind, those with eye diseases, and all who seek Christ's light amid darkness.

shortBio: Roman martyr traditionally remembered as a soldier of Christ whose courage under persecution made him a lasting emblem of steadfast witness.
quote: I have always adored the Lord Jesus Christ. I worship Him now, and I will never cease to worship Him.
patronages: He is especially honored among soldiers, athletes, and those who seek perseverance under suffering and opposition.

shortBio: Bishop of Myra whose pastoral charity, defense of the faith, and legendary generosity made him one of the most widely loved saints in Christian memory.
quote: The giver of every good and perfect gift has called upon us to mimic His giving, by grace, through faith, and this is not of ourselves.
patronages: He is patron of children, sailors, merchants, and all who practice hidden generosity and trust in God's providence.

shortBio: Martyr saint revered throughout East and West as a witness of courage, military fidelity, and fearless confession of Christ in the face of evil.
quote: I am a soldier of Christ. I cannot stop fighting.
patronages: He is especially honored among soldiers, scouts, and all who seek courage in spiritual and earthly combat.

shortBio: Young mother and martyr of Carthage whose prison diary remains one of the most vivid early Christian witnesses to courage, faith, and joyful surrender to Christ.
quote: I am a Christian, and nothing that is not in accordance with the truth can be uttered by my mouth.
patronages: She is especially honored among mothers, martyrs, and all who seek courage to remain faithful to Christ under persecution.

shortBio: Bishop and martyr of the apostolic age, disciple of Saint John, whose steadfast confession linked the Church directly to the witness of the apostles.
quote: Eighty-six years I have served Him, and He has done me no wrong. How can I blaspheme my King who saved me?
patronages: He is especially honored among bishops, martyrs, and all who seek perseverance in the apostolic faith unto death.

shortBio: Philosopher, apologist, and martyr of the early Church whose reasoned defense of Christianity remains one of the clearest windows into second-century Catholic life and worship.
quote: We who formerly delighted in fornication now embrace chastity alone; we who formerly used magical arts dedicated ourselves to the good and unbegotten God.
patronages: He is especially honored among philosophers, apologists, and all who seek to defend the faith with reason, courage, and charity.

shortBio: Early pope and apostolic father whose letter to the Corinthians stands among the Church's earliest witnesses to ordered ecclesial charity and authority.
quote: Let us fix our gaze on the Blood of Christ and realize how precious it is to the Father.
patronages: He is especially honored among pastors and all who seek fidelity to apostolic order, unity, and peace in the Church.

shortBio: Bishop, apostolic father, and martyr whose letters on the way to Rome gave the Church enduring teaching on Eucharist, unity, and courageous desire for Christ.
quote: I am the wheat of God, and am ground by the teeth of the wild beasts, that I may be found the pure bread of Christ.
patronages: He is especially honored among bishops, martyrs, and all who seek Eucharistic faith and fearless love for Christ.

shortBio: Pope and Doctor of the Church whose preaching and doctrinal clarity helped articulate the Church's faith in Christ and defend Roman primacy in a turbulent age.
quote: Christian, recognize your dignity and, now that you share in God's own nature, do not return to your former base condition by sinning.
patronages: He is especially honored among pastors, preachers, and all who seek firmness in doctrine joined to confidence in divine grace.

shortBio: Bishop, theologian, and Doctor of the Church whose Trinitarian preaching and contemplative depth made him one of the most luminous voices of the Greek Fathers.
quote: If you are a theologian, you truly pray. If you truly pray, you are a theologian.
patronages: He is especially honored among theologians, preachers, and all who seek the union of prayer, doctrine, and contemplative truth.

shortBio: Bishop and theologian of the Cappadocian tradition whose mystical depth and doctrinal precision helped shape the Church's understanding of God, creation, and the spiritual life.
quote: Concepts create idols; only wonder comprehends anything.
patronages: He is especially honored among theologians and contemplatives who seek a deeper grasp of divine mystery through wonder and holiness.

aliases: The Harp of the Holy Spirit
shortBio: Deacon, poet, and Doctor of the Church whose hymns and teaching gave the Syriac tradition one of its greatest voices of repentance, praise, and biblical imagination.
quote: Virtues are formed by prayer. Prayer preserves temperance.
patronages: He is especially honored among poets, hymnographers, and all who seek to praise God with doctrinal beauty and penitential prayer.

shortBio: Bishop and Doctor of the Church whose defense of Nicene faith in the Latin West made him one of the Church's key anti-Arian witnesses.
quote: Everything that happened to Christ lets us know that, after the bath of water, the Holy Spirit swoops down upon us from high heaven.
patronages: He is especially honored among bishops, theologians, and all who seek courage in defending the truth of the Trinity.

shortBio: Lawyer, statesman, martyr, and humanist scholar whose fidelity to conscience and the unity of the Church cost him his life under Henry VIII.
quote: The things, good Lord, that we pray for, give us the grace to labour for.
patronages: He is especially honored among lawyers, statesmen, public servants, and all who seek integrity of conscience under pressure.

shortBio: Augustinian bishop renowned for preaching, reform, and generous love for the poor, remembered as a shepherd whose charity matched his learning.
quote: If you want God to hear your prayers, hear the voice of the poor.
patronages: He is especially honored among bishops, preachers, and those who serve the poor with practical charity and pastoral zeal.

shortBio: Priest and university professor whose charity, intellectual seriousness, and patience made him a model for Christian scholarship and pastoral humility.
quote: Fight all error, but do it with good humor, patience, kindness, and love.
patronages: He is especially honored among teachers, students, and all who seek holiness through learning joined to humility and kindness.

shortBio: Monk, reformer, cardinal, and Doctor of the Church whose austere holiness and fearless counsel served the purification of the Church in a difficult age.
quote: The cell of the monk is the Red Sea through which the soul passes in fleeing from the Pharaoh of sin.
patronages: He is especially honored among reformers, contemplatives, and all who seek zeal for holiness in personal and ecclesial life.

shortBio: Dominican canonist and confessor whose legal wisdom, pastoral prudence, and missionary concern made him a major servant of the Church's sacramental and juridical life.
quote: A confessor must have the ear of a doctor, the heart of a mother, and the patience of a saint.
patronages: He is especially honored among canon lawyers, confessors, and those entrusted with wise and merciful pastoral judgment.

shortBio: Mercedarian priest remembered for ransoming captives, heroic endurance, and complete generosity in risking himself for the freedom of others.
quote: I would rather be a slave than let a single soul perish for want of the faith.
patronages: He is especially honored among expectant mothers, infants, and all who seek God's help in childbirth, captivity, and danger.

shortBio: Franciscan friar of great simplicity whose humility, obedience, and mystical prayer made him one of the Church's most famous saints of ecstatic contemplation.
quote: O my God, I do not deserve these favors; how can someone as stupid and ignorant as I fly?
patronages: He is especially honored among students, air travelers, and all who seek humility, simplicity, and trust in God beyond their natural ability.

shortBio: Franciscan missionary whose preaching, music, and joyful austerity made him a luminous evangelizer across South America.
quote: With God's grace and a violin, one can win any heart.
patronages: He is especially honored among missionaries, evangelists, and all who seek to draw hearts to Christ with joy, sacrifice, and creativity.

shortBio: Augustinian preacher and reformer remembered for confronting violence and corruption with fearless preaching, peacemaking, and holiness of life.
quote: Better to die than to tolerate injustice.
patronages: He is especially honored among preachers and all who seek courage in confronting injustice with truth and peace.

shortBio: Priest, teacher, and spiritual guide of Turin remembered for forming clergy, consoling prisoners, and preparing the condemned to meet God with hope.
quote: Paradise, paradise! How beautiful it will be to go to paradise!
patronages: He is especially honored among prison chaplains, confessors, and all who minister mercy to the suffering and condemned.

shortBio: Priest and founder devoted to the formation of poor and working youth, remembered for combining social concern with priestly tenderness and educational realism.
quote: The greatest work of charity is to teach the poor to help themselves.
patronages: He is especially honored among youth workers, educators, and all who serve the poor through practical and dignified forms of charity.

shortBio: Priest and founder of the Little House of Divine Providence, remembered for radical trust in God while serving the sick, poor, disabled, and abandoned.
quote: Providence will provide; my only capital is trust in God.
patronages: He is especially honored among hospital workers, the disabled, and all who seek to rely on divine Providence while serving the most vulnerable.

shortBio: Marist missionary and martyr whose patient witness and hidden charity bore fruit in Oceania through the offering of his life for the Gospel.
quote: Dying for Christ is not losing one's life; it is finding it.
patronages: He is especially honored among missionaries, Oceania, and all who seek steadfast charity in difficult or resistant apostolic work.

shortBio: Italian priest and founder devoted to the Holy Face of Jesus, remembered for parish ministry, sacramental fidelity, and patient spiritual reparation.
quote: To look upon the Holy Face of Jesus is the surest way to repentance and holiness.
patronages: He is especially honored among parish priests and all who seek repentance and holiness through devotion to the Holy Face of Christ.

shortBio: Capuchin lay brother whose life of simplicity, joyful poverty, and ceaseless gratitude made him a beloved model of hidden holiness.
quote: Deo gratias!
patronages: He is especially honored among lay brothers, the poor, and all who seek joy, humility, and gratitude in ordinary service.

shortBio: Russian ascetic and mystic revered for radiant holiness, prayer, and spiritual counsel centered on peace, repentance, and the Holy Spirit.
quote: Acquire the Spirit of Peace and a thousand souls around you will be saved.
patronages: He is especially honored among contemplatives and all who seek interior peace, prayer of the heart, and compassionate spiritual fatherhood.

shortBio: Early bishop of Como remembered for defending orthodox Christological teaching and shepherding the Church in northern Italy with pastoral courage and fidelity.
quote: Christ is true God and true man, and the Church must confess him without confusion or division.
patronages: He is especially honored in Como and among Catholics devoted to episcopal fidelity, orthodox teaching, and steadfast pastoral leadership.
